ux_issues.description: AI created a full 5-day itinerary but did not add the pre-booked Sagrada Familia tour as its own dedicated Day 3 itinerary item. It referenced the tour in the description of the Day 3 shopping item ('After the Sagrada Familia...') but the tour itself is missing from the itinerary items. For an organized user like Priya who wants a single source of truth, pre-booked activities should appear explicitly in the itinerary.
ux_issues.suggestion: When building an itinerary around existing bookings, the AI should insert booked activities as their own itinerary items at the correct time slot rather than only referencing them in other items' descriptions.

ux_issues.description: AI response in the tool loop sometimes produces duplicated confirmation text (e.g., 'I've already added those two recommendations to your itinerary in my last response'). This happens because the tool loop continues after the first tool result and the AI restates what it already said. While not harmful, it reads as slightly confused to the user.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider tuning the system prompt to avoid restating already-confirmed tool actions when the tool loop continues.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: When building the 5-day itinerary, the AI did not integrate the Sagrada Familia booking (June 17, 10:00 AM) as a standalone itinerary item on Day 3. The booking data was available and the AI referenced it in text, but did not create a corresponding create_itinerary_items entry for it.
ai_behavior_issues.context: The persona explicitly asked to build a 5-day itinerary incorporating existing bookings. The AI created 15 new items and correctly placed arrival/check-in/checkout/Montserrat but omitted the Sagrada Familia as a discrete item.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The booking ingestion and retrieval pipeline is excellent — all three bookings (flight, hotel, activity) were parsed correctly with accurate confirmation codes, provider names, dates, and details. The AI accurately answered specific questions about terminal numbers, check-in times, and tour details from ingested booking data. ExtractBookingField worked flawlessly across all three booking types. The recommend_booking tool triggered correctly for bookable activities and included the FTC affiliate disclosure. Restaurant recommendations correctly did not trigger recommend_booking. The AI respected the 'don't drink much' preference throughout. The main gap was that the AI did not add the pre-booked Sagrada Familia tour as its own itinerary item when building the full 5-day plan, which is a moderate issue for an organized user wanting a complete view. No expert persona handoff was triggered during this session, which was not a primary test vector for this persona but would have been a nice touch for architecture discussions.
